nodes.label: The Golden Loop
nodes.rationale: Never code without a written plan; a phase isn't done until tests are green AND you've seen it run; ship something runnable each phase — the repeating cycle Plan→Build→Test→Demo→Verify→Update tracker→Confirm→Next phase keeps every phase both proven and visible.

nodes.label: 3-Layer Tracking System
nodes.rationale: Three separate views of the same work (PLAN.md vision, per-phase spec, PROGRESS.md status) are kept in sync so build status is never a mystery — any session, machine, or teammate resumes instantly from committed files rather than memory.

nodes.label: Definition of Done (DoD)
nodes.rationale: Each phase lists its exit criteria before work starts so 'done' is objective, not a vibe — enforced by /arc-phase-done which refuses to close a phase without green tests, a live demo, and an updated tracker.

nodes.label: Walking Skeleton (Phase 0)
nodes.rationale: Phase 0 wires the whole flow end-to-end on fake data before adding depth, de-risking the architecture on day one; phases are then ordered by risk, not ease, so the part that could kill the project is proven earliest.

nodes.label: Offline-First / Interface+Fake+Real Pattern
nodes.rationale: An interface + fake + real implementation for every external dependency enables a stub mode with no keys or network — fast dev, deterministic tests, free CI — while real integrations stay optional until needed.

nodes.label: Change Intake
nodes.rationale: A mid-build change, idea, or suggestion — even the AI's own — is never coded ad-hoc; it is routed through triage (current-phase note / new phase spec / ADR / bug fix) and the tracker updated first, so the tracker never drifts out of sync with the code.

nodes.label: Quality & Safety Gates
nodes.rationale: Definition of Done, tests-per-feature, golden/eval sets, a security & privacy pass, cost/budget ceilings, observability, idempotency/resumability, and a rollback/kill switch are treated as non-skippable gates rather than optional polish.
